rulings.Case Title: NANCY EASTHAM et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment was continued to allow defendant to take the deposition of Mr. Cole, which was noticed for December 16 but has not yet occurred. The hearing on the motion is continued to February 2, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. Mr. Cole¿s deposition must occur no later than January 20. Supplemental reply papers may be filed no later than January 28. If anyone contests this tentative ruling, the hearing will occur at 9 a.m., not 9:30am. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DARLENE BALL et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Graybar Electric Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Mr. Eisele¿s declaration (submitted by plaintiffs) and his deposition testimony (submitted by defendant) create a triable issue whether Mr. Ball was exposed to asbestos-containing insulated wire supplied by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Eisele¿s declaration are overruled. The hearing on this motion will be Friday January 7, 2011 at 10 a.m. However, anyone wishing to contest the tentative must send an email by 4:30pm today to contestasbestostr@sftc.org. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE MCMILLIAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Defendant Borg-Warner Corporation To Be Deemed Dismissed, Or In The Alternative, To Have Discovery Re-Opened To Allow Borg-Warner To Disclose Expert Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Borg-Warner Corp. by its successor-in-interest Borg Warner Morse TEC Inc.¿s motion to be deemed dismissed is denied and its alternative motion to re-open discovery is granted only as to defendant Borg Warner. The court will entertain an oral motion to continue the trial date at the trial call on January 5, 2011 at 1:30pm. The hearing on this motion will occur at 9 a.m., not 9:30 am. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANITA BISHOP VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION, LTD. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Nacco Materials Handling Group, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. By not presenting contention discovery responses with its moving papers, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Ms. Bishop was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant. The evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply papers was not considered. The hearing on this motion will occur at 9am, not 9:30am. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN HALL JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Union Carbide¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 and 6-11 (sophisticated intermediary and bulk supplier defenses, loss of consortium and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation). The sophisticated intermediary defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). As to punitive damages, there are triable issues whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiff dismissed her cause of action for false representation. The hearing on this motion will be Friday January 7, 2011 at 10 a.m. However, anyone wishing to contest the tentative must send an email by 4:30pm today to contestasbestostr@sftc.org. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN HALL JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Elementis Chemicals Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Hall was exposed to asbestos-containing joint compound distributed by defendant. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Mann¿s declaration are sustained. The court did not consider defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication because it failed to comply with the requirements of CRC 3.1350(b) & (d). The hearing on this motion will be Friday January 7, 2011 at 10 a.m. However, anyone wishing to contest the tentative must send an email by 4:30pm today to contestasbestostr@sftc.org. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIE HOWARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiff an opportunity, if he is able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. The hearing on the motion will occur at 9am, not 9:30am. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY LIMA VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 5, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant The Sherwin-Williams Company¿s amended demurrer to plaintiff¿s amended complaint will be heard in Dept. 604 at 9:00 a.m. Hon. Marla J. Miller presiding. = (220/MJM)
